site.title: Riigikogulased
site.tagline: The Riigikogu in numbers: party discipline, speeches, spending and more.

members.heading: Party Discipline
members.subheading: Discipline percentage measures how often a member votes with their faction's majority position. Procedural votes (presence checks, agenda adoption) are excluded.
members.empty: No data yet. Run the scraper to populate the database.

hub.heading: The Riigikogu in numbers
hub.intro: The standout figures of the current Riigikogu. Tap a card for the full picture.
hub.rebelSub: voted against the faction line {d} times out of {c}
hub.closestSub: that is how close the outcome came to flipping
hub.sigSub: their most distinctive word — click to read

decisive.heading: Closest votes
decisive.intro: The closest calls: times when votes cast against the faction line were numerically enough to change the outcome — the result held only because those votes did not fall the same way. Each vote is checked against its correct passage threshold: usually a simple majority of votes cast, but no-confidence motions, proposals to the Government, immunity waivers and the laws listed in §104 of the Constitution require a majority of the full membership (51 votes).
decisive.emptyBody: In this Riigikogu, votes cast against the faction line have not changed the outcome of a single vote. Defections occurred in {defectionVotes} votes, but every outcome would have been the same had everyone followed their faction line.
decisive.flippedHeadline: {n} votes where a defection changed the outcome
decisive.closeIntro: Votes where the defectors were numerous enough to flip the result — the outcome held only because those votes did not fall in one direction.
decisive.methodNote: Method: only substantive votes count (presence checks and agenda adoptions are excluded). The faction line is the majority position of the faction's members; a member counts when their vote (yes/no/abstain) differs from it. The passage threshold is derived from the vote type and bill title — the API does not expose it directly.

statistika.speakersIntro: Counts of floor speeches, questions, and procedural remarks in plenary (Riigikogu statistics).
statistika.searchIntro: Search every member's plenary speeches — who has said what.
statistika.boardNote: Member of the Riigikogu board: short procedural remarks made while presiding (calling speakers, etc.) are not counted as speeches, so this figure reflects time spent presiding.
statistika.tenureNewNote: Joined the Riigikogu recently (under 3 months); a low speech count is expected.
statistika.modeAbsoluteHint: Raw totals over the whole term.
statistika.modeRateHint: Speeches per month, normalized by time served in the Riigikogu.
statistika.tenureFlagNote: Members with under 3 months served: the rate is computed but not ranked, because too small a window gives a misleadingly high rate.
statistika.expensesIntro: Each MP's annual expense-compensation limit and how much of it was used (Riigikogu published summaries).

varia.hubIntro: Lighter statistics about Riigikogu members — attendance, generations, word use and more.
varia.tileKohalolekDesc: Who is absent from votes most often?
varia.tilePolvkonnadDesc: Age distribution of members by faction.
varia.tileMargusonadDesc: Each faction's most distinctive words in speeches.
varia.absenceIntro: The share of substantive votes a member was absent for. Presence checks and agenda adoptions are excluded; members with under 20 votes are omitted.
varia.generationsIntro: The distribution of members' birth years and the age profile of each faction. Click a bar to see who belongs to that age group.
varia.margusonadIntro: Each faction's most distinctive words in plenary speeches (TF-IDF over lemmatised stenograms). Click a word to see the speech excerpts where it was used.
varia.tileFraktsioonidDesc: Unity, attendance and size in the 15th Riigikogu.
varia.tileKuludDesc: MPs' expense allowance limits and annual usage.
varia.tileOtsustavadDesc: Did voting against the faction ever change a result?
varia.wordHint: Click to see speeches using this word
varia.excludedNote: For transparency: TF-IDF surfaced these but we dropped them by hand (empty filler words, plenary procedure, forms of address, and name fragments):
varia.tileInimesedDesc: Hobbies, professions, universities, children and birthplaces.
varia.tileVorgustikDesc: Friendship groups and cause caucuses across factions.
varia.inimesedIntro: The background of Riigikogu members: hobbies, pre-politics profession, universities, children and birthplaces. Source: members' public profile pages.
varia.hobbiesSub: How many members named each field as a hobby – click a word to see who.
varia.universitiesSub: How many members studied at each Estonian university.
varia.childrenSub: The number of children in the Riigikogu.
varia.birthplaceSub: Where Riigikogu members were born (villages mapped to their county seat).
varia.vorgustikIntro: Members' parliamentary friendship groups (with other countries) and cause caucuses (cross-faction thematic groups).
varia.friendshipSub: How many members belong to each country's group.
varia.globetrotterSub: Members belonging to the most friendship groups.
varia.causesSub: Thematic groups and their cross-faction reach.

metoodika.title: Methodology
metoodika.intro: How we compute party discipline and where the data comes from.
metoodika.s1p: All voting and member data comes from the Riigikogu's official open data API (api.riigikogu.ee, licensed CC-BY-SA 3.0). The Riigikogu API only records faction (fraktsioon) membership, not party membership — party membership comes from the Estonian Business Register's registry of political parties, matched to members by name and date of birth. Data is refreshed once a day.
metoodika.s2h: Whose line a member is scored against
metoodika.s2p: For each vote we assign the member a "scoring party": the party of the faction they sit in at the time of the vote; if they are unaffiliated but a registered member of a party, that party. Truly independent members (no faction and no party) are excluded.
metoodika.s3p: A party's position on a vote is the strict majority choice (yes / no / abstain) among its faction members. The member's own ballot is excluded from the majority calculation when they themselves sit in that faction. If no choice has a strict majority — the faction was split — the vote does not affect the member's score.
metoodika.s4p: We exclude procedural votes (presence checks, agenda adoption), votes where the member was absent (absence is not a defection), votes where the faction was split, and votes where the member's party has no faction in the Riigikogu (there is no line to defy).
metoodika.s5p: Discipline percentage = votes aligned with the party line / (aligned + defecting votes). A score of 95% means that out of a hundred counted votes, the member voted against their party's majority five times.
metoodika.s6p: The majority position may not always match the party's official stance — we measure behaviour, not agreements. For members with few counted votes (e.g. recently seated substitutes) the percentage is statistically unstable; leaderboards therefore apply a minimum-vote floor.

nonSitting.heading: Won a seat but never took it
nonSitting.intro: Candidates who won a mandate but never entered the Riigikogu (stayed on as minister, MEP, or mayor, like Mihhail Kõlvart). Ranked by personal votes.
nonSitting.name: Name

factions.heading: Factions (experimental)
factions.intro: Faction cohesion (discipline), attendance, and size in the 15th Riigikogu.

memberDetail.speechesNote: Source: Riigikogu speech statistics (whole XV term).
memberDetail.boardNote: Short procedural remarks made while presiding (calling speakers, opening votes, etc.) are not counted as speeches, so this figure reflects time spent presiding.
memberDetail.tenureNote: This member has served only a short time this term, so a low speech count is expected.
memberDetail.searchHint: Search is Estonian inflection-aware (e.g. "kool" also finds "koolis", "koolide").
memberDetail.searchNoResults: No speeches found for this search.
memberDetail.searchTotal: {n, plural, one {1 speech found} other {# speeches found}} · newest first

mandateNote.personal: Won a full quota of votes outright.
mandateNote.compensation: Entered parliament via the party list.
mandateNote.substitute: Did not win a 2023 mandate; entered the Riigikogu as a substitute.
mandateNote.notElected: Ran in 2023 but was not elected.
